title: These 4 Top Dividend Stocks Have 22% to 47% Upside Potential, According to Wall Street
text: The share prices of many REITs have fallen well below their estimated value. The sell-off has also pushed up dividend yields.

title: Alexandria: Cheap By Association
text: Alexandria's fundamentals have continued their upward trajectory. The market price has sunk.

title: 4% Yield, 34% Below NAV, And Down 42%, We're Buying Alexandria Real Estate
text: Fundamentals remain strong. Fear appears to be elevated. There are legitimate macro risks, but they're smaller for ARE than for many other stocks. We came across quite a few viable explanations for the dip. They can have a big bearing on market sentiment, but not on fundamentals. Great REIT at a low price.

title: Are You Looking for a High-Growth Dividend Stock?
text: Dividends are one of the best benefits to being a shareholder, but finding a great dividend stock is no easy task. Does Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) have what it takes?

title: The More It Drops, The More I Buy
text: REITs are down very significantly over the past weeks. We continue to execute our accumulation strategy.

title: Want to Invest in Real Estate With Less Stress? Try This REIT.
text: Alexandria Real Estate Equities is a leading provider of life sciences office space. This real estate investment trust also has outperformed the greater market since its 1997 IPO.

title: Dividend Champion, Contender, And Challenger Highlights: Week Of March 26
text: A weekly summary of dividend activity for Dividend Champions, Contenders, and Challengers. Companies which changed their dividends.

title: Yields Plunge, REITs Crater
text: Tired of hearing "rates up, REITs down?" Same here. There are more factors to consider. Treasury yields are falling as more investors recognize the death of inflation and call for rates to be cut by the end of the year.

title: Alexandria Real Estate: My Oh My, This Is A Strong Buy
text: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. is an Office REIT, though there are reasons why I would argue this description doesn't 100% fit the company as such. It's "better" than your standard Office REIT. I own a small stake in Alexandria - but I'm about to want a whole lot more, given the company's current valuation and its quality.

title: The State Of REITs: March 2023 Edition
text: The REIT sector gave up most of January's big gains in February with a -6.18% total return. Small cap (-7.42%) and mid cap (-6.05%) REITs fell the furthest in February, with micro caps (-5.15%) and large caps (-4.74%) seeing smaller declines.

title: After Plunging -25.2% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why the Trend Might Reverse for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE)
text: The heavy selling pressure might have exhausted for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) as it is technically in oversold territory now. In addition to this technical measure, strong agreement among Wall Street analysts in revising earnings estimates higher indicates that the stock is ripe for a trend reversal.

title: Very Good News For REIT Investors
text: We just had two major bank failures, but deposits should be safe. Future interest rate expectations are now lower and this is very good news for REITs.

title: 13 Ideal Inflation-Busting March Dividend Dogs
text: March-April, 2022, UBS, Kiplinger, and Barrons analysts and reporters combined to list five sets of stocks sorted as: “Inflation Busters.” Those five articles revealed 48 equities, with 43 paying dividends.

title: Fed Fights 'Ghost Of Inflation Past'
text: U.S. equity markets oscillated forcefully while interest rates plunged this week as contagion from a pair of mid-sized bank collapses spread to other lenders while authorities scrambled to contain the fallout. On brand with the "March Madness" theme this week, the S&P 500 seesawed between gains and losses in each session but ultimately ended the week higher by 1.1%.

title: Net Lease REITs Are A Strong Buy Even Amid High Inflation And Rising Interest Rates
text: Net lease REITs would probably benefit on net from a recession this year. I briefly pitch my five favorite net lease REITs right now.

title: Alexandria Real Estate: Have No Fear, High Quality Is Here
text: Alexandria Real Estate is hitting on all cylinders with near-record leasing activity and rent spreads. It has a strong line of sight with respect to tenant health and potential for future expansion.

title: Healthcare REITs: Life After The Pandemic
text: For Healthcare REITs - the physical epicenter of the pandemic - the road to recovery has remained inconsistent across its distinct sub-sectors, with "private-pay" segments now showing notable improvement while "public-pay" segments relapse. Public-pay segments - Hospital and Skilled-Nursing - have seen a re-intensification of tenant operator issues amid pressure from soaring labor costs and waning government fiscal support, triggering some missed rents and lease renegotiations.

title: Alexandria Real Estate: A Blue Chip REIT With Solid Upside
text: Alexandria is not your typical office REIT as its unique focus on life sciences and biotech makes it resilient against the work-from-home trend. Moreover its growth prospects have high visible with a highly leased development pipeline, rent escalation clauses and no refinancing risk.

title: Warm Winter For Global Economy
text: U.S. equity markets rebounded following their worst week of the year as investors parsed data showing surprising resilience in global economic activity this winter despite stiff interest rate headwinds. Snapping a three-week skid and notching its best week since late January, the S&P 500 advanced 2.0% this week, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 rallied nearly 3%.

title: 5 Years From Now, You'll Probably Wish You Grabbed These REITs
text: REITs are priced at the lowest valuations in years. Even high-quality REITs trade at large discounts to net asset values.

title: Losers Of REIT Earnings Season
text: After covering the Winners of REIT Earnings Season earlier this week, Part 2 of our Earnings Recap focuses on the worst-performing property sectors and common threads shared by these laggards. While there were upside standouts and impressive reports within these lagging property sectors, the losers of REIT earnings season included: Office, Technology, Net Lease, Regional Malls, Healthcare, and Specialty REITs.
